subject: Adjust DelaySeconds parameter to ensure uui-server can start normally
commit_message:
Adjust DelaySeconds parameter to ensure uui-server can start normally

When we switch to https, the startup time is slightly longer. The initial DelaySeconds time that
was originally set is more critical. Sometimes it may cause our program to restart because the
time limit for health check has been reached, so we need to adjust this parameter.
Because it is the modification caused by the https switch change, so I used this issue Id.

Issue-ID: USECASEUI-356
Change-Id: I929814cd586db55f040588dffe2f18832f409c92
Signed-off-by: hekeguang <hekeguang@chinamobile.com>
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

ok so extending the timers was not the right solution, there was a architecture issue leading to resource over consuming, extending timers was just hidding the issues.

If you have fixed the problem of resource management, self release a new version of your UUI, then create a new patch in OOM referencing this new UUI version.
It will trigger automatically a gating chain, it means that it will reinstall an ONAP and perform test for you to give you the feedback if it is working or not (before any merge).

To summarize 
- create new image version (in a first step you can use a staging image even if the JobBuilder will vote -1 as OOM master accepts only release versions) but it will trigger a gating to check if your fix solves the issue or not
- if it solves => self-release
- amend the patch with the staging version to use a release version
- if everything is fine, it will be merged - so we will have done our integration job, meaning we will have provided you feedback before merge and ensured the stability of the master branch
see https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/OOM+Gating for details

Note if your code change fixes the issue, this specific patch could be abandonned (useless workaround)
BTW Do you have any robot healthcheck tests available? each component is supposed to have some in order to improve the quality of the gating.
